title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will miss the Celtics' game against the Pelicans due to right Achilles repair management.
Jaylen Brown, managing left Achilles tendinitis, is set to play against New Orleans despite being listed as questionable.
Three other Celtics starters were marked as probable but will be available for the game against the Pelicans.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, a 49-year-old from Concord, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for her daughter Emma.
Emma, 16, was diagnosed with medulloblastoma on April 4, 2025, requiring immediate surgery and extensive treatment.
Lori aims to honor Emma's bravery during her cancer treatment by running the marathon, highlighting her journey and future hopes.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens won't debut for the Bruins against Tampa Bay as they aim to clinch a playoff spot.
Coach Marco Sturm emphasizes patience with Hagens, wanting him adjusted to the system before making his debut.
The Bruins have three regular-season games remaining, but it's uncertain if Hagens will play before postseason.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes released a list ranking 250 self-made Americans, featuring Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers.
Another Forbes list highlights historical self-made figures with Massachusetts ties, including Benjamin Franklin, Louisa May Alcott, and Babe Ruth.
Forbes used "Self-Made Scores" and various sources, like archives and expert judges, to evaluate financial success and impact for the lists.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will take place on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending at Copley Square.
This year's marathon will feature 30,000 participants from over 130 countries, with six start waves instead of four.
Live coverage will be available on WCVB Channel 5 and live streaming through the Very Local app starting at 4 a.m.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots are hiring Shane Bowen as an analyst, formerly a defensive coordinator for the Giants and Titans.
Shane Bowen previously worked with Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel at the Titans and Ohio State.
Defensive staff changes include Zak Kuhr as coordinator and Terrell Williams returning as assistant head coach.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston has various public restroom options, including libraries, firehouses, and some restaurants, as noted by the city's Age Strong Commission.
An interactive map by the Age Strong Commission helps locate public restrooms across Boston neighborhoods, emphasizing their public health importance.
Massachusetts law allows people with certain medical conditions access to employee-only restrooms in specific retail locations under specific criteria.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins, a freshman at Masconomet Regional High School, alleges inadequate response to harassment and threats of sexual violence.
Teachers claim the administration ignored reports of gender discrimination and failed to act against a golf coach and student athletes.
Superintendent Michael Harvey disputes claims of insufficient response, citing privacy regulations, while School Committee mulls next steps.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers have paid over $137 million extra for gasoline since the Iran conflict began in February.
Gasoline prices in the U.S. doubled, with costs surpassing $4 per gallon, impacting Massachusetts residents significantly.
Proposed suspensions of state gas tax are debated; Massachusetts governor opposes, while some states temporarily suspend taxes.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Canoe River Campground in Mansfield offers sites during World Cup matches with rates from $51 to $187 a night.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres in Carver has over 100 campsites available in June and July, with glamping options.
Boston hotels raise prices for World Cup, while FIFA reduces hotel bookings due to inflated original estimates.
